_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
The Climate Change Authority Act 2011 (No. 143, 2011) establishes the Climate Change Authority (CCA) as an independent Commonwealth statutory body. It does not directly regulate freight forwarders; rather it sits at the apex of Australia's climate governance architecture, producing binding-quality advice and statutory reviews that cascade obligations downstream through connected legislation — principally the National Greenhouse and Energy Reporting Act 2007 (NGER Act) and the Carbon Credits (Carbon Farming Initiative) Act 2011 (CFI Act/ACCU Scheme).

In supply-chain role taxonomy terms, the workspace sits as a **downstream data subject and indirect compliance actor**. The CCA's mandatory five-yearly review of the NGER Act sets the measurement methodology and reporting thresholds that determine whether a freight operator must register directly with the Clean Energy Regulator (CER). The workspace is unlikely to exceed NGER registration thresholds (currently ~50 kt CO₂-e Scope 1+2 per year) as a freight forwarder that does not own fleet; however the workspace is increasingly captured as a **Scope 3 data supplier** to clients who are Group 1 mandatory sustainability reporters under the ASRS regime — a regime explicitly designed to complement, not replace, NGER. Legal confirmation is required on two points: (a) whether the workspace or any consolidated parent entity crosses NGER registration thresholds via operational control of warehousing or ground-handling facilities; and (b) whether any humanitarian cargo operations conducted under government contracts create a separate Commonwealth reporting obligation.

The Act's Part 3 (Special Reviews, s.59) authorises the Climate Change Minister or Parliament to direct the CCA to conduct bespoke reviews on any climate issue, including transport sector decarbonisation pathways. The 2024 Sector Pathways Review, delivered under this power, explicitly modelled freight and heavy-vehicle emissions and identified air and road freight as sectors with significant near-term abatement obligation. This gives the CCA's outputs quasi-regulatory weight: government Net Zero Plans and sector plans are developed in response to CCA advice, making the Act the upstream source of policy that will ultimately shape fuel standards, clean-energy incentives, and Safeguard Mechanism baselines relevant to the workspace's contracted carriers.

*Source: Climate Change Authority Act 2011 (No. 143, 2011), Commonwealth of Australia, 27 November 2011. https://www.legislation.gov.au/Details/C2011A00143. Climate Change Authority Act 2011, Climate Change Laws of the World, climate-laws.org. NGER scheme overview, DCCEEW, April 2026. https://www.dcceew.gov.au/climate-change/emissions-reporting/national-greenhouse-energy-reporting-scheme*
